channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Liberty look to close out series against Lynx and clinch franchise's first WNBA championship
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The New York Liberty are one victory away from securing the franchise's first WNBA championship. They'll have their first chance Friday night when they face the Minnesota Lynx in Game 4 of the Finals.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: University of South Florida to support hurricane relief efforts during UAB football game
item.description: Fans who donate three or more shelf-stable items will receive a coupon for two complimentary tickets to a 2024 home football game of their choice.
item.dc:creator: Merissa Lynn, USF, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dodgers star Mookie Betts sends warning shot to Mets, who are on brink of elimination
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are one win away from winning the NL pennant after another dominant performance, and Mookie Betts plans to deliver the knockout punch on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tony Bennett cites NIL and transfer portal era as reason he's suddenly retiring at Virginia
item.description: CHARLOTTESVILLE, Va. (AP) — Describing himself as “a square peg in a round hole,” a tearful Tony Bennett said Friday he suddenly retired from coaching at Virginia because he wasn’t suited to navigate the current landscape of college basketball.
item.dc:creator: MIKE BARBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler charged with DUI, possession of handgun after traffic accident
item.description: FRANKLIN, Tenn. (AP) — Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler was arrested and charged with driving under the influence and possession of a handgun after a minor collision on Thursday. Cutler, 41, was given a $5,000 bond and was released, according to a statement from the Franklin Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prep spotlight: Andrew Hill’s Hawaii trip brings gratitude, sets stage for strong volleyball year
item.description: Prep spotlight: Castro Valley water polo wants to face the best, Kerri Walsh Jennings sizes up Mitty-St. Francis rivalry, Alameda defender is intense competitor, Cousins' bond carries Granada, Miramonte players take trip to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, Joseph Dycus, Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: Budding rivalry: Starting Saturday, Stanford and SMU will see a lot of each other due to ACC scheduling quirk
item.description: Stanford has faced SMU just once before Saturday, but the two new ACC schools will meet every season for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Harold Gutmann, The Mercury News

item.title: Chiefs president admits he doesn't necessarily agree with Harrison Butker's political views
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs president Mark Donovan said Thursday he didn't "necessarily agree" with Harrison Butker's commencement speech from earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travis Kelce passionately defends Guardians fandom after team's thrilling win
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs star Travis Kelce gave a passionate defense of his Cleveland Guardians fandom as questions arose about who he was supporting in the MLB postseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohtani and Betts lead Dodgers to latest blowout of Mets for 3-1 lead in lopsided NLCS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fresh off another October blowout, Shohei Ohtani and the Los Angeles Dodgers are one win from the World Series.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Pendrith shoots 61 in first start since the Presidents Cup and leads in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Taylor Pendrith took two weeks off after the Presidents Cup and didn't miss a beat, matching his career-low on the PGA Tour with a 10-under 61 that gave him a three-shot lead in the Shriners Children's Open on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legendary musical group to attend Mets' NLCS game, perform star player's walk-up song live
item.description: The Temptations will be on hand for Game 5 of the National League Championship Series to perform "My Girl," Francisco Lindor's walk-up song.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anaheim knocks off Utah in overtime, 5-4
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) - After winning two overtime games to begin its first road trip, the Utah Hockey Club closed out the trip with an overtime loss.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Shohei Ohtani homers as the Dodgers rout the Mets 8-0 for a 2-1 lead in the NLCS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — By the time Shohei Ohtani stepped to the plate in the eighth inning Wednesday night, the Los Angeles Dodgers were already in control. Once the ball left his bat, Game 3 of the National League Championship Series was over.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Cole Kmet earns NFC Special Teams Player of the Week honors
item.description: Cole Kmet was named NFC Special Teams Player of the Week for Week 6 after successfully snapping on all five of the Bears' point-after attempts during their game in London against the Jacksonville Jaguars.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Former SJSU basketball standout found dead in Romania
item.description: (KRON) -- Former San Jose State University Women's Basketball standout Tyra Whitehead has passed away, according to a social media post from SJSU Women's Basketball. Whitehead was a forward who went on to become a WNBA prospect with the Washington Mystics.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Yankees beat Guardians 6-3 for 2-0 ALCS lead
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Aaron Judge sent the ball soaring toward Monument Park and Gleyber Torres tagged up at first base. “That was disrespect out of Gleyber, man,” Judge said with a smile. “He’s seen me hit 58 of those things this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: CMU football aims to win fourth straight conference game on homecoming
item.description: It's time for CMU homecoming and the Cowboys of New Mexico Highlands come in confident following their first win of the season
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Bills acquire wide receiver Amari Cooper from Browns, giving QB Josh Allen a much-needed playmaker
item.description: Amari Cooper went from a fading No. 1 wide receiver in Cleveland to a top one with Buffalo. Looking to add a playmaker for quarterback Josh Allen, the Bills acquired Cooper on Tuesday in a trade with the skidding Browns, whose season seems to be unraveling by the week with the NFL’s worst offense.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ricky Pearsall returns to the 49ers practice for the first time since shooting
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — San Francisco 49ers rookie receiver Ricky Pearsall returned to the field for practice on Monday for the first time since he was shot in the chest during an attempted robbery nine days before the season opener.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers' Flaherty shares NLCS Game 1 'moment' with mother
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers' major trade-deadline acquisition from this summer is already worth it. Jack Flaherty was the star of the show in Game 1 of the NLCS between L.A. and the New York Mets, as he pitched an electric seven shutout innings while giving up just two hits in a 9-0 win.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Aidan Hutchinson likely down 4-6 months with broken leg, Lions coach Dan Campbell says
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Aidan Hutchinson is likely done playing this year because of the leg injury he sustained against the Cowboys, but surgery to repair his fractured tibia and fibula “went great” and Lions coach Dan Campbell said Monday he isn't ready to declare his star pass rusher out for the
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs with Bowles looks back at hurricane worries and one big win
item.description: Buccaneers Head Coach Todd Bowles stayed up most of the night last Wednesday into Thursday morning watching Hurricane Milton consume the Tampa Bay area with damaging high winds, torrential rain, and flood waters claiming spots that had barely begun recovery from Hurricane Helene less than two weeks
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn't have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn't help but note the irony. The actor is being added to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame’s James F.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashes world record by nearly 2 minutes at Chicago Marathon
item.description: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashed the world record by nearly two minutes at the Chicago Marathon on Sunday, winning in 2:09:56.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks beat Oilers, 5-2, for first win of 2024-25 season
item.description: EDMONTON, Alberta (AP) — The Blackhawks will go into the final game of their season-opening road trip with win on their ledger.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
